annotator S.report_text:
The spear-phishing emails used in this attack resemble file-sharing notifications from OneDrive. The emails contain a link to a legitimate, but compromised third-party website Adversaries may send spearphishing emails with a malicious link in an attempt to gain access to victim systems.
Adversaries may compromise access to third-party web services.
When users click the link An adversary may rely upon a user clicking a malicious link in order to gain execution.
they are served a ZIP archive containing a malicious LNK file.
It executes an obfuscated PowerShell command that extracts a base64-encoded payload from within the LNK file itself Adversaries may abuse PowerShell commands and scripts for execution.
Adversaries may attempt to make an executable or file difficult to discover or analyze by encrypting, encoding, or otherwise obfuscating its contents on the system or in transit.
The first-stage DLL, cyzfc.dat, is created by the PowerShell script in the path %AppData%\Local\cyzfc.dat. It is a 64-bit DLL that exports one function: PointFunctionCall. Adversaries may inject dynamic-link libraries (DLLs).
After connecting to the first-stage command-and-control server at pandorasong[.]com (95.216.59.92), cyzfc.dat begins to install the final payload `cyzfc.dat` extracts and installs the final payload.
The PowerShell script then executes cyzfc.dat by calling rundll32.exe. Adversaries may abuse rundll32.exe to proxy execution of malicious code.
key 0xC5. De-XOR of the second-stage payload with a rotating XOR (ROR1), starting at byte 0xC5.
That payload is XORed with the same XORing algorithm used for reading.
When decrypted, it forms a PE file with a Meterpreter header, interpreting instructions in the PE header and moving control to a reflective loader: Adversaries may use Obfuscated Files or Information to hide artifacts of an intrusion from analysis. They may require separate mechanisms to decode or deobfuscate that information depending on how they intend to use it.
Adversaries may execute malicious payloads via loading shared modules.
Adversaries may reflectively load code into a process in order to conceal the execution of malicious payloads.
The third payload eventually gets loaded and connects to the command-andcontrol (C&C) server address that is baked-in inside configuration information in the PE file. Adversaries may communicate using OSI application layer protocols.
This configuration information is de-XORed at the third payload runtime: Adversaries may use Obfuscated Files or Information to hide artifacts of an intrusion from analysis. They may require separate mechanisms to decode or deobfuscate that information depending on how they intend to use it.
CobaltStrike is a feature-rich penetration testing tool that provides remote attackers with a wide range of capabilities, including escalating privileges, capturing user input, executing arbitrary commands through PowerShell or WMI, performing reconnaissance, communicating with C&C servers over various protocols, and downloading and installing additional malware.

annotator L.report_text:
CozyBear

The spear-phishing emails mimicked sharing notifications from OneDrive.

If recipients clicked a link on the spear-phishing emails they began an exploitation chain that resulted in the implantation of a DLL backdoor that gave the attackers remote access to the recipients' machines.

The emails contain a link to a legitimate, but compromised third-party website. users click the link they are served a ZIP archive containing a malicious LNK file. It executes an obfuscated PowerShell command that extracts a base64-encoded payload from within the LNK file itself, cyzfc.dat, is created by the PowerShell script in the path %AppData%\Local\cyzfc.dat. The PowerShell script then executes cyzfc.dat by calling rundll32.exe. cyzfc.dat begins to install the final payload by taking the following actions De-XOR the second-stage payload with a rolling XOR (ROR1), starting from key 0xC5. The second stage is an instance of Cobalt Strike Meterpreter That payload is XORed with the same XORing algorithm used for reading The third payload eventually gets loaded and connects to the command-andcontrol (C&C) server address that is baked-in inside configuration information in the PE file.
